channel.title: ContinuousIntegration on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in ContinuousIntegration on emsenn.net

item.title: DevOps and Continuous Delivery as Planning Infrastructure
item.description: DevOps is a set of practices that unifies software development (Dev) and IT operations (Ops) to shorten the development lifecycle and deliver software changes more frequently. The term emerged around 2008–2009, with Patrick Debois organizing the first DevOpsDays conference in Ghent, Belgium, in 2009. The foundational texts are Jez Humble and David Farley’s Continuous Delivery (Addison-Wesley, 2010) and Gene Kim, Patrick Debois, John Willis, and Jez Humble’s The DevOps Handbook (IT Revolution Press, 2016).
